sentences.text: Hello?
Bye!
How are you?
Well, how are you guys?
Doing well.
How's your Monday going so far?
Busy, but good.
Yeah.
Common theme for a Monday, it seems like.
Yes.
I did just get a text from Saad that he's gonna be a few behind.
I don't know if he reached out to you as well, but….
All good.
We can probably get started because I've seen the product, but yeah, thanks for hopping on our call.

So, Rebecca and I are from the Poetic team, and Poetic, we are the Internal preferred marketing agency of Asset Living, and so we work on a lot of different properties across multifamily and student housing.
One of the things that we've been working on on our end is just, like, our protocol for … You know, having our website to be visible to our AI engines.
And so we're working on just, like, our product there.

And then, in the mix of that kind of due diligence, we understood that, like, a lot of… some asset living properties have already started with Maven AI, and so we thought, like, maybe there's some synergy here that we can start to combine the products together.
So, but in order to do that, we just… wanted to just understand, like, learn a little bit more about Maven AI, if you wouldn't mind giving us the tutorial.
Yeah, of course.
Happy to jump in and show you what we're doing, the different products we offer.
We've been partnering with the asset team mostly on our Google Business tool, sounds like you're familiar with that.
Hmm.
I can just start with introducing myself.
We did meet very briefly at Social Media Summit, I believe, but….
Dude?
Yes, very briefly at, like, happy hour, I think, like, the day before it kicked off.
Excellent.
Well then, good to see you again.
Yeah, but I'm… I'm Kylie, I've been working with the Asset team for a couple years now.
I work to support our largest customers across the country, specifically with the Maven AI products.
And I'm Morgan, it's nice to meet you.

I was not at SMS this year, but I am on the customer success team here, been around for about 5 years, managing the asset account for about 4, so pretty familiar with the whole portfolio and the structure of how things go, and I'm just more of your, like, go-to, managing your account day-to-day, reporting, everything that you need for implementation, setups, check-ins, stuff like that.
Good to meet you, Brian and Rebecca.
I'm Patrick.
I lead the sales team.
So I've been with the company about 9 years now, so excited to be chatting.
been familiar with Poetic for… quite a while, so excited to see if there's some potential synergies here.
Yeah.
Who wants to go on our side?
Saad?
Do they know you already?
We know Sad.
Things I saw it.
today with them, so they don't need to hear from me, yeah.

I'm Rebecca, and I am actually responsible for finance and operations within Poetic, but, in a strange convergence, I… my career actually started doing AI software development, and so I've obviously been very excited and, and, … interested in moving our products forward in this LLM space.
And then I'm Brian, I'm the SVP of Product and Strategy, and so I lead our product teams across digital marketing, websites, and creative.
Awesome.
Well, nice to meet you guys.

So we're thinking for the call today to just do… it sounds like you've seen a little bit of the product, you're a little familiar with us, so we were thinking to do, like, high-level overview of the reasons behind the product, jump in and show just a couple live examples.
Think it makes most sense to show, like, one live asset property that we have on Google.
And then if you guys wanted to touch on the social product as well, could do, like, a high-level overview there.
Is there anything else that you wanted to add into that, into the agenda for the day?
No, Saad, are people using the Maven AI product on the social side as well within NASAD?
Okay.
Yeah, so, social, I think there's maybe a few properties that are about to try this on, but it's really the Google.
Aspect that we have now about to have 102, 103 properties now.
And what I was alluding to earlier, we just had a conversation with David Walters about an MSA with these guys as well, this morning.
So, Brian, I know I jumped on a little late, but I think you already set the stage of why I made this introduction, so I'll just stop talking and won't repeat myself, so….
Awesome.
Well, before we jump in, I just wanted to ask a couple questions around what you guys are doing right now, and how it compares to the automated tool that we're partnering with you all on.
So, what products do you guys currently offer, as far as automation or optimizing the Google profiles, or social profiles?
Yeah, I don't know if it's so much in the automation side of it, but one thing that we're focused on is just, like, content of the Google Business Profile.

From our SEO standpoint, with our SEO customers, what we do is we identify a specific keyword that we want to start ranking for, and then we start posting content, first on a blog of the website, but then also, like.
Creating relevant contact for the Google Business profile.
We haven't figured out how to automate yet, just yet.
Candidly, but that part of it is, like, just posting on the Google Business Profile, and also just utilizing… we utilize SEMrush.
Yeah.
Just transparent to help manage, like, local listings management for our properties.
Yeah.
So those are, like, the two kind of Venn diagram overlaps that I see at this point right now.
Nice.
And what's most interesting… Yeah, what's interesting with your side is how you're doing the real-time pricing and availability in your Google Business Prof.
That's particularly interesting to us.
Yeah.
So, from your side and Rebecca, that's usually… I mean, posting is really important, of course, but usually our… a headline feature, is that price and availability?
So, from what you're saying, Brian, is it like an SEO tier?
You don't have to get into too much detail, but then you have a team that manually would kind of create posts as a service for your properties at Upton?
That's correct, that's correct.

We find that a lot of properties don't have the team or the bandwidth to write content, and so that's where our team probably kind of connects the digital marketing side of the keyword research and, like, where we want to be with the content creation side.
And with our SEO packaging.
So you could maybe take that list of keywords, and I'm sure you utilize ChatGPT, too, to create some… a little faster content, but you still have to do it at some manual prompting.
And then, Rebecca, from your side, with the price and availability, have you done anything?
With trying to get that, either a manual process or any of that content onto Google in the past.
We're not doing it automatically… we do it automatically on our website.

We have our own integrations on our website, which, I mean, you'll probably get into this, so I don't want to jump the gun, but I'm curious, are you pulling your real-time pricing and availability from the website, the property website, or are you pulling it straight from the PMS?
the PMS.
Oh, good, okay, yeah.
So that's what we're doing on the website, but we are not doing it to automate the GBP.
Information.
It's not a… it's not a scrape from the website that then feeds over to Google, it's a… it's a direct… Integration with the syndication feed.
bet.
So….
Yeah, that's really cool.

And then, Brian, I think you're right, like, a lot of our customers and, we worked with some agencies, but a lot of our customers are thinking about this new world with LLMs and what they prioritize in their… when they're searching for information, and it sounds like you're focused on websites as well, to make sure they're discoverable, more so in this world of LLMs, and we think there's a big overlap with what Google's doing on the profile, and that content being important for AI overviews, and Gemini, and even Local Search as well.
So it's kind of the background of this product.
We agree, we agree with that.
We're in… yeah, we agree with that wholeheartedly.
GBP is part of our AEO offering, yep.
Especially if Gemini wins, I can imagine Google Business.
Right?
All these acronyms, y'all.
Yeah, A-E-O-A-I-O-G-O.
E-I-E-I-O.
The amount of posts I'm seeing online, people are like, SEO is dead.
I'm like, it's not dead, alright?
Let's not overreact, SEO's not dead, but everybody's pushing GEO and AEO.
So, we'll kind of show you what… how we're thinking about it, and see if there's a potential here as well.
Well, I….

Well, I have one more question, sorry, and… and forgive me if this is too forward, but, you know, poetic, is it… is it just… Is it serving asset, or are you… More so than the asset portfolio as well in the industry.
We do third-party also.
We do non-ask.
You do.
You do?
Okay, cool.
That's what I thought, I just wasn't sure.
Alright, thanks.
Cool, well, I'll jump in, and share what it's all about.
So… Sounds like we already got through this part, but really, all of the tools that we have are built to automate processes that your teams are currently doing.
So, just what you said, our teams don't really have the bandwidth or the capacity to be creating content for all of these profiles.
That's why customers sign on with us.
Is to automate the process of content creation and posting, and fully optimize your profiles.
So that's what this tool solves, is both time savings, but then also increasing exposure for these properties with consistent content being fed to them.
We do have a few different products, under the Maven name.
So, the one that we've been partnering with, asset for the most properties on is the Google Business AI tool.
We also have a social posting tool that we're just starting to partner with some asset properties on.
So similar concept to Google, it's automated post syndicated directly from the feed to your social profiles.
And then we have a reputation and a listings management tool as well.
Are you DMing as well, or are you just posting?
Just the posting, just organic posts.
And then, like we've mentioned, really all of this is powered by that feed syndication.
So, syndicate with all the major PMSs, and that's the source of truth for all of the information we're pulling.
Photos, amenity-based information, pricing, availability.
Our implementation team also does look through the websites when they're implementing, in case there's any additional photography or amenity-based information.
They'll pull it in at that time, but it's really the feed where we're getting this content.
And then jumping into the Google Business AI tool specifically.
So we are automating multiple areas throughout the profile, one of them being daily automated posts.
Grabbing the photos and the amenity keywords directly from the feed, but then our AI tool layers on top of that to create the content, and I'll show you an example of what that looks like as well.
Then the products feed that we were talking about, that's that live pricing and availability feed, direct from the syndication, and updating at least once a day.
I know you guys use Yardi.
With Rent Cafe, we can pull 4 times a day, so we ping as often as the feed allows us to make sure we have the most up-to-date and accurate information.
RealPage 2?
With RealPage, yep, yeah.
Yeah, Yardi, RealPage, Entrata, Resman, all of the main ones.
And then, we also can pull concession data.

So pulling that, like everything else, directly from the feed, and then within the posts, we're posting concessions, and that's a dynamic post, so we can have that fall off as soon as the concession's removed, so we're not storing any outdated concession data there.
And then we automate the Q&A.
So, we've had a lot of customers over the last for 6 months tell us that they've been really focusing on, like, FAQ type of content on their websites.
So, this is the same thing, automating this section on the profile, and also keeping it updated every single day.
Can you… can you touch on that again?
What are you doing with FAQ pages?
Yeah, let me show you a live example.
So this is a property, an asset property that we're working with.
Let me show you what the FAQ looks like.
Essentially, we are seeding and answering, some of the most frequently asked Q&A questions right on the profile here.
So, we're asking questions around amenities, pet policies, office hours, concessions, availability.
And where are you getting those questions from?
Are you integrated with a chatbot, or…?
We created these questions, so we have a list of, like, 30 questions that we pull from.
So we post it, and then our tool automatically answers it, and the answer is updated at least once a day.
And how does the tool get the information?
Is that scraping the website?
From… directly from the feed.
So, so from the beginning….
Right.
From the GPT?
Yep.

From the feed, we get your… We get your pictures, we get your list, generally a list of amenities, and so from there, because we have the information, we can create this in a question and answer format.
If we see that there's an EV charging amenity, we can say, does this property have EV charging?
And the answer is yes.
So, it's a pretty cool, for the first company to do this in the industry, way to be proactive with your Q&A.
And then, you know, Google loves Fresh.
accurate, up-to-date content.
We have this on a live feed, so every day it's updated.
Are the questions changing?
No.
Okay.
So the questions don't change unless this is removed from the feed.
For whatever reason, if you got rid of a pool, probably wouldn't happen.
We are paying the fee to if that question changes, and you take that amenity away, then this question will go away.
And out of that list of 30-some questions that we're asking, we're matching it to your property.
So we're only asking the amenity-based questions that match your property.
If you didn't have a pool, we're not asking about the pool.
We just want to highlight amenities you do have, and availability that you do have.
Yup.
And then, are you, … hmm, okay.
So, with FAQ pages, you also have to have the content in your schema markup.
So, like, this is just optimizing your GBP, but not necessarily for LLMs on the website, right?
Right, yep.
Yeah.
Yeah, just, just the Google profile, the same concept, but go ahead.
But… but it is updating Wait, can it update the FAQ page on the web… the website?
No, no, they're strictly managing the Google business profile, so, the website itself would be, whether it's the digital firm, or the actual marketing manager or somebody on-site team.
Yeah, I'm just trying to think what an LLM would do with that.
If you have an FAQ page on your website, and you have FAQs in your Google business profile, which one… which one reigns?
I don't know.
You are definitely touching on a potential roadmap item for us, though.
I don't know.

If we can, if we can automate FAQs to websites for our customers, For now, it's, … Yeah, it's… we've seen some of these answers actually be delivered in AI overviews sporadically, but… We think if… our general posture with Google is if there is a section on the profile where you can have information about your property, you should absolutely do it.
And so this is another place for… Optimization and updates to be taking place.
Well, we would be one of the partners.
You probably should reach out to about the… hosting the website, because actually we're not at Lakewood Ranch is actually a poetic hosting, so….
Yep.
Yeah, we might have to… we've been asked this a couple times, … I know it's something that's on top of our… product team's mind, builds the FAQs for websites.
Yeah, because obviously that's part of our AEO offering, is uploading the FAQs on the website.
And how would you do that now?
Would you have to manually do that across the board, or…?
Well, we're… we're lucky that we use a CMS that really can do that pretty dynamically.
Yeah, that can do, like, the… we can update the content and then have it flow through to the schema markups pretty dynamically.
Okay.
so we're… we're pretty lucky.
I think if we had our websites based on, like, WordPress or something, it would have been a terribly manual process.
F.
What's the… what's the LMS software you use?
Which way?
the LMS tool that you use?
the LMS….
The CMS tool is working.
Sorry, CNS, excuse me, CNS.
No, it's all good.
It's worthwhile.
LMS, CMS.
the web flow.
I was mixing LLM and CMS into LMS.
Too many acronyms, see?
I know.
I think that the more interesting flow, it depends on which way you turn the water on, like.
goes from your Google to our website, or our website to the Google profile.
I mean, I think….
Right.
There's a lot of ways that I could point.
Yeah.
Yeah, but it just seems like they could get out of sync.
Like, if you've got FAQ in one spot and another, and then not knowing which one is actually informing the LLM, I, I don't know.
And then that could change over time, too, which one is actually Feeding properly.
God.
Interesting.
Thank you.
Yeah.
Going back to the profile itself, I want to show the other areas of the profile.
Oh, I've done too many searches here.
Let me just retype it in.
Nada… Yo.
I do want to show the other areas that we're automating throughout the profile, one of them being those daily posts.
So, every day we're creating a new, unique post, grabbing the amenity keywords and the photos from the feed, but then we have our AI tool layers on top of that to create the actual content.
So, every day, the post will have a photo, the content will match that… that photo.
So, it'll be calling out exactly what's in the photo.
And then in here is where we're displaying the concessions.
So you saw the concessions in the Q&A, we also have the concessions displayed here within the posts.
And this is also… this is a dynamic post, so we have this displayed in a way that as soon as the concession's removed from your feed, then this post will fall off.
So, a lot of these posts highlighting amenities throughout the property, in-unit, community-level amenities.
And… Morgan, I know when we were together at the summit for the multifamily team, we also talked about, potentially highlighting location.
So, like, based on the neighborhood page, like, if there's certain businesses that we've highlighted, you know, having posts around that.
So it's not just amenities and floor plans, right?
It's, like, other content that we push out there, too.
Yep.
Yeah, there's a variety of posts that we can do, both in social and Google.
And one way we can kind of put some variation into the posts is through, SEO keywords.
So I know you talked about keywords on the front end.
We can also intake lists of SEO keywords, enter those on the backend, so when these posts go out, they're including those keywords.
Nice.
So a lot of customers will use this for exactly what you just said, Saad.

Obviously, we're going to be pulling the city name already, so the city name will be in these posts, but if you wanted to… to note that it's downtown, or it's in a certain suburb, or things that are unique about the property that we just can't grab from the feed, we can do that here, to make these a little bit more custom.
Usually, customers can send us, like, a brand guideline.
I'm sure if you're working with properties, you might have, like, a brand voice guideline, or….
Or something like that.
Oftentimes, our customers send us that doc, and we can integrate that into the LLM.
Really?
You can do personas for different properties?
Yeah, I mean, we'll take as much data as we can at the property level to prompt the model, yeah.
I mean, I… I'm sure some are more detailed than others, but… most of our customers, like Kylie said, they lean towards, like, hey, we want to highlight Our location, or…
maybe we have one-bedrooms that we're trying to push, we want to highlight those in our posts.
Any type of keywords, we can make sure they're in the text for the posting.
Yep.
Then, you'll probably get to this, but do you mind if I ask, like, what type of reporting that you're sharing back to the properties?
Yep, so we have, we use Watergraph, so it shows increase in traffic over time.
So, I did pull up the asset one.
So, we're able to show, this is specific to the Google tool, change in traffic.
So, this is the last year of data to the prior year.
And we've been working a lot with Shelby's team on the new dev properties, so some of these numbers will be very high because they weren't doing anything before.
But we are seeing an increase across all, student, new dev, student new dev, and the conventional we've been working with.
But this is what the reporting looks like.
So we can show change in traffic for map impressions, search impression, website clicks, phone and driving.
Yep.
And then, obviously, we can break that down by property as well.
In portfolio, so if you guys wanted a little bit more customization in the reporting, we can add tabs for each different portfolio type.

We can break things down not just by property, but also by city or state, so there's a lot of ways that we can give you some additional metrics in the reporting, but overall, you'll see, like, a roll-up of the metrics, and then Below that, we'll give you some additional data points.

Morgan, Kylie, would there be a world, since Poetic is owned by ASSA, for any asset properties you guys have on your portfolio, for us to get this data integrated into our digital marketing packets, with the reports that Poetic's already putting together, for the PPC campaigns, social ad campaigns, all that stuff.
Mmm….
I don't have to answer today, just something to think about.
I think that was, like, my bigger purpose of.
Canada.
Kind of putting you both into a room, … is because, like, obviously, both are impacting everything that is on the digital front, right?
So, like, how do we… How to find a way to, like, merge these relationships to then have one seamless packet of reporting and data that we can all work off of and actually work to improve.
Yeah, I think….
Yeah, like, we'll see what we can do there.

I think, … whether it's sharing these reports with your team, and have, you know, giving you these numbers, you can download these into PDFs in different ways to actually put it into a packet, or… you know, we're pulling all of this directly from your Google Analytics, so whatever you're already seeing in Google Analytics, it'll be the same numbers that you see here, it's just gonna be in a little bit of a different format.
So, in that way, we already do share the data, but we can definitely figure out how to… how to put it into a report for you guys.

Well, I know that Brian and Saad and I, we talked… talked about the genesis for this call was, as Poetic is about to roll up… roll out our answer engine optimization, or generative answer engine optimization, whatever you want to call it.

You know, a piece of that is… is obviously an SEO piece, which we now sell a la carte, that would be part of a bundle, because, of course, you can't optimize for LLMs unless you control the website, you're doing the SEO work, and obviously then doing the structured data and content work on the website.

So, … what you guys are doing on the GBP side is something… like, we don't have that automated, and we would love to have that automated, like y'all are doing, and so I wonder if there's a unique opportunity to… Combine pieces and parts to Offer, you know, a really great solution, with the two… with the two combinations.

I think so, like, we've, we've, … largely work directly with, like, the asset live-in crew, but we have a large third-party operator where we work with their digital team, which is basically their agency.
For that portfolio.
And what they did is they've wrapped our product into their baseline SEO package for their partners.
They don't say it's Maven product, they say it's their product, it's like a white label, but they've… basically taken our tool to sell back to their portfolio, so….
Yeah, Patrick, I'm glad… I'm glad you're picking up what we're putting down, and that's kind of what we wanted to….
Yeah.
Really figure out an opportunity there, because, like.
cow.
hey, I want to scale your product, because… Kylie and Morgan just are awesome people, and they always deliver on the stuff.
But then I just see an opportunity for us, right, from what Poetic is doing already, and there's a gap here with what you guys are doing, like, if we can package this all up.
and get that service rollout across the board, I mean, that's a huge opportunity for all parties involved, so….
Yeah.
Oh, go ahead, Rebecca.
Yeah, no, and just so you know, today we're hosting just under 1,000 websites of asset websites, and so to superimpose what percentage of those websites want to participate in the LLM search economy?
You know, so the distribution would be there.
Okay, that was gonna be my next question, like, from our point, of course, this is very obvious, but we want to work with as many asset properties as possible.
What we didn't know coming in today is, like, what's the… the path?
Is it through Poetic, who has more of a penetration within the portfolio?
Is it more so direct to the properties?
I know there's varying levels of marketing support each property gets.
Maybe, Saad, that's something that you and I… we can all talk about.
What was it like?
But we do do some business this way through large portfolios that have an internal agency, and they tell us the same thing.
Here's the adoption that we have, and then they use our technology to replace what was once a manual process.
They had marketing coordinators manually post.
They completely took that away and put in our software to handle all of that.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That makes a lot of sense.
Yeah, and Patrick, that's why I wanted to have the initial call with David.

Just so you understand that we are serious about this partnership, but then I wanted you to have this call to understand the scale and what you have the potential to tap into with the poetic partnership being leveraged into that, all under that umbrella.
So hopefully that gives you a little bit more to work with when you guys are discussing, kind of, the pricing model and that partnership scope, accordingly.
Yeah, that's helpful.
Because this wouldn't just be asset, right?
Because Poetic doesn't just do asset properties, they're doing this across the nation, so… So again, just opening up more opportunities there for you guys as well.
We think the product… the partnership could be scalable.
scale of properties counts, and also scale product, what we were talking about before, in terms of, like, the FAQs.
Yeah.
the generation and the posting, like, I think that's where there's a lot of synergy, too.
Whether we do the generation, or you do the generation, and probably getting into both places.

Do you… out of curiosity, do you… do you partner with any, like, other technologies to help power aspects of your business, or is this kind of the first foray into, let's look at an outside partner to maybe help us out here?
I mean, not on the… not on the DM side, not on the digital marketing side.
Sure.
I mean, I guess you could argue our hosting and support, our partnership with Webflow, is quite deep.
Sure, yeah, yeah.
But not on the digital marketing side, other than tools that we use, but I mean, not on other people's tech that we deliver services for.
Okay.
And then I think, Saad, obviously from our conversations, we've got a lot of internal conversations.
I'm gonna bring in our CFO, too, to just work through what we can get in front of you for a scale of pricing.
On your end, Rebecca and Brian, are you guys planning for your 2026 product offerings?
Is that… Where you're at, or how fast are you looking to potentially Roll out a solution like this, if we landed on this being the… a good fit for… for us.
I don't want to jump.
We plan to roll out our AEO product offering is available September 1st.
We're introducing asset marketing team to it first, and then rolling out from there.

But we know that… we're feeling the sense of urgency because we also know these properties are doing their budgeting in the fall right now for next year, and so we want to get this out there and get this in the property budgets.
for our, AEO product, because it's obviously a bigger price tag for them.
Yeah.
Yeah, Patrick, I think a week and a half ago, I was on a call with Poetic.
I'm on weekly calls with these guys, and they mentioned that they were rolling this out, and I immediately brought up y'all's name.

I said, hold on, hold on, we have somebody who does this, maybe you could find a way to marriage this and, you know, have an even more, full-service option there, so… So yeah, time is certainly of the essence.
That doesn't mean we want to rush… rush that relationship, but again, … Your name was the one that was first brought up, and that's why we're on here today.
Well, we appreciate that, Saad.

Based on this combo, I agree, I think there's a lot of… potential here, so are you open to us taking just a little bit of time and circling with our CFO and getting something in front of you then, San, from here?
Yep.
Yeah, so I think now that you understand, kind of, just how much we want to scale with you guys.
enough.
what opportunities do we have under the asset umbrella, which includes the poetic team, right?

So, and again, from a technical standpoint, for all the back-end stuff, I want to make sure Brian and Rebecca are connected, you know, on how we would go more so on that path, but… I'm just… consider me the broker.
I just wanted to put it in front of the right people, and … you know, so….
I think I've done my job tonight.
Yeah, you sure have, and we appreciate it.
Hey, Patrick.
You mentioned there was another owner-operator that was white-labeling Maven AI.
Are you able to share who that is?
I'd love to see what that looks like on their property websites.
I mean, on their property Google searches.
I think it's… am I okay to say it, Kylie?
I think it's fine.
Our PM does.
Oh, okay, RPM, okay.
Yeah, so what they do is they have They have an internal digital team that used to be agencies they work with, they took everything in-house.
And then, ….
So we power all of their Google Business optimization.
For their portfolio.
Is there a particular property website that comes to mind that I could… you would recommend we look at?
Riley, you know more than me.
Yeah, Radian San Diego is one of theirs that I use for demoing.
rate… radian.
Radian, R-A-D-I-A-N, in San Diego.
Cool.
Yeah, the profile will look just like the one that I just demoed, because we're powering every… every piece of their… of their profile.
So, thank you.
Cool.
The only piece that we didn't get to today was that products feed.
Oh, yeah.
I'm gonna share it really quick before we hop off, because it is the piece of this that we get the most excitement from out of companies like Asset.
So… pulling in pricing availability, update it at least once a day.
Like I said, with Rent Cafe, we can pull, like, 4 times a day, to pull floor plan level pricing and availability.
